evals.prompt: The product team changed one requirement midstream. Update the spec in place, keep the old requirement visible as superseded, and tell me what downstream artifacts need rerun.
evals.expected_output: Amends the spec without deleting the original requirement history and identifies affected follow-on artifacts.
expectations: Marks the changed requirement as superseded or replaced.
Calls out downstream artifacts that must be updated.

evals.prompt: A plan is now inconsistent with the spec. Govern the change so the plan, tasks, and validation stay aligned instead of drifting apart.
evals.expected_output: Forces downstream consistency and does not allow the mismatch to remain unaddressed.

evals.prompt: We need to revise the requirement, but I do not want the earlier completed tasks erased. Update the workflow carefully and preserve history.
evals.expected_output: Preserves completed work, records the amendment, and avoids a destructive rewrite of the spec history.
expectations: Preserves completed tasks or prior state.
Uses in-place amendment rather than replacement.
Reports any unresolved contradictions or stale artifacts.
